role_title: Chief of Staff to the Chief Product Officer

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in Product, Business Operations, Strategy, or a Chief of Staff function
Comp range $224K-$280K base consistent with senior-level individual contributor at a late-stage tech company
Reports directly to Chief Product Officer; main point of contact between DRD and rest of company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in Product, Business Operations, Strategy, or a Chief of Staff function — ideally in a high-growth tech company
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in a high-growth tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A track record of running complex cross-functional programs, not just tracking them
Strong EQ and the ability to build trust across all levels of the organization, including executives.
Comfort with ambiguity and an instinct for identifying what matters before anyone else names it
Exceptional communication skills — you can write a crisp exec brief and give sharp verbal feedback in the same afternoon
Ability to hold context across many workstreams and know when to push, when to pause, and when to escalate
Presence and credibility in the room, even when you don't hold the title
Successful candidates will have extremely high EQ and IQ, with an ability to see the big picture while having an obsession with the details.

scope.surface: Chief Product Officer's office; Product organization including product, design, engineering, and delivery (DRD)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS for private markets (venture capital, private equity, private credit)
primary_metrics_inferred: Product org operational cadence and rhythm of business
Clarity and alignment across DRD and rest of company
core_responsibilities: Run the Product Org Rhythm of Business: drive operational activities, set and own cadences and forums including R&D Reviews, Leadership meetings, organization-wide All Hands
Own underlying infrastructure to support the organization including resource planning and budgets
Execute on Leadership Direction: cascade decisions through the organization, surface to the CPO what she needs to know before she asks
Create Leverage on Strategic Projects: own connective tissue on high-ambiguity, cross-functional projects including integration readouts for acquisitions and high-coordination product rollouts
Act as a Sounding Board for the Leadership Team: ask questions that help people arrive at cleaner answers faster
Be the Voice of the Product Org Across Carta: own inputs and outputs connecting Product org to the rest of Carta

compensation.equity_form: equity for all full time roles

green_flags: Compensation range clearly disclosed ($224K-$280K base) and competitive for the role level
Equity included for all full-time roles
Highly visible role reporting directly to the CPO with access to executive forums
Concrete examples of actual problems given (acquisitions, roadmap vs. marketing, scope reduction conversations) — unusual transparency about real challenges
Named CPO (Vrushali Paunikar) signals genuine investment in the role and clear reporting relationship
Carta is a scaled, well-recognized company (65,000+ companies, $185B AUM on platform)
red_flags: Location appears to be San Francisco onsite only based on comp disclosure; no explicit remote or hybrid policy stated in body text — candidates outside SF should confirm
Visa sponsorship policy not stated — unclear for international candidates
Role is described as operating at high ambiguity and navigating 'unprecedented times' — may signal organizational instability or frequent context switching
No mention of team support (e.g., CoS team, analysts) — role may be a solo function with broad scope

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + reporting line + years required)
location.type set to 'onsite' inferred from SF-only comp disclosure; body does not explicitly state hybrid or remote eligibility
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship set to 'unclear' — not addressed in JD body beyond E-Verify mention
ambiguities: Location policy is ambiguous: compensation is listed only for San Francisco, implying onsite or SF-based hybrid, but the JD body never explicitly states work arrangement. Treated as onsite but candidates should confirm.
Visa sponsorship: JD mentions E-Verify usage but does not state whether sponsorship is offered or not. Left as 'unclear'.

The 'ideally in a high-growth tech company' qualifier on years of experience is soft language but the requirement itself (8+ years) is presented as a hard requirement. Classified as hard with the employer-type qualifier noted.
